failed_cases.description: Public names import, default Markdown is callable, explicit renderers construct through the public API, and TOC projection is exposed.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown, HTMLRenderer, ASTRenderer, escape_html
m = Markdown()
print(callable(m), hasattr(m, 'parse'), hasattr(m, 'toc'), hasattr(m, 'register_inline'), hasattr(m, 'register_block'))
print(type(Markdown(renderer=HTMLRenderer()).renderer).__name__)
print(Markdown(renderer='ast')('plain')[0]['type'])
print(escape_html('"<&>'))
failed_cases.expected_output:
True True True True True
HTMLRenderer
paragraph
"<&>
failed_cases.actual:
True True False True True
HTMLRenderer
paragraph
"<&>
failed_cases.expected:
True True True True True
HTMLRenderer
paragraph
"<&>

failed_cases.description: Block parsing recognizes ATX heading level, source text, and the public generated anchor id for an isolated heading.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
t = Markdown('ast').parse('# Alpha & Beta!')[0]
print(t['type'], t.get('level'), t.get('text'), t.get('attrs', {}).get('id'))

failed_cases.description: HTML renderer emits core block tags, including heading anchors, from direct public token dictionaries.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import HTMLRenderer
tokens = [{'type':'heading','level':2,'attrs':{'id':'hi-there'},'children':[{'type':'text','text':'Hi'}]},{'type':'thematic_break'},{'type':'block_code','text':'x < y','lang':'py'}]
print(HTMLRenderer().render(tokens))

failed_cases.description: Heading AST ids, rendered HTML anchors, and TOC entries project the same inline-derived heading text and duplicate slug state.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
doc = '# API & **SDK**

## API `SDK`

# API SDK'
html = Markdown()(doc)
ast = Markdown('ast')(doc)
toc = Markdown().toc(doc)
heads = [t for t in ast if t['type'] == 'heading']
print([h['attrs']['id'] for h in heads])
print([entry['id'] for entry in toc])
print([entry['text'] for entry in toc])
print('id="api-sdk"' in html, 'id="api-sdk-2"' in html, 'id="api-sdk-3"' in html)
failed_cases.expected_output:
['api-sdk', 'api-sdk-2', 'api-sdk-3']
['api-sdk', 'api-sdk-2', 'api-sdk-3']
['API & SDK', 'API SDK', 'API SDK']
True True True
failed_cases.expected:
['api-sdk', 'api-sdk-2', 'api-sdk-3']
['api-sdk', 'api-sdk-2', 'api-sdk-3']
['API & SDK', 'API SDK', 'API SDK']
True True True

all_cases.description: Public names import, default Markdown is callable, explicit renderers construct through the public API, and TOC projection is exposed.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown, HTMLRenderer, ASTRenderer, escape_html
m = Markdown()
print(callable(m), hasattr(m, 'parse'), hasattr(m, 'toc'), hasattr(m, 'register_inline'), hasattr(m, 'register_block'))
print(type(Markdown(renderer=HTMLRenderer()).renderer).__name__)
print(Markdown(renderer='ast')('plain')[0]['type'])
print(escape_html('"<&>'))
all_cases.expected_output:
True True True True True
HTMLRenderer
paragraph
"<&>
all_cases.actual:
True True False True True
HTMLRenderer
paragraph

all_cases.expected:

all_cases.description: Block parsing recognizes ATX heading level, source text, and the public generated anchor id for an isolated heading.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
t = Markdown('ast').parse('# Alpha & Beta!')[0]
print(t['type'], t.get('level'), t.get('text'), t.get('attrs', {}).get('id'))

all_cases.description: Block parsing recognizes fenced code blocks with an optional language.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
t = Markdown('ast').parse('```python\nprint("<x>")\n```')[0]
print(t['type'], t.get('lang'), t.get('text'))

all_cases.description: Block parsing recognizes indented code and thematic breaks.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
tokens = Markdown('ast').parse('    a < b\n    c\n\n---')
print(tokens[0]['type'], tokens[0]['text'].startswith('a < b\nc'))
print(tokens[1]['type'])

all_cases.description: Block parsing recognizes unordered and ordered list structures.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
tokens = Markdown('ast').parse('- one
- two

1. first
2. second')
print([(t['ordered'], [i.get('text') for i in t['items']]) for t in tokens])

all_cases.description: Block parsing recognizes block quote nesting as block content.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
quote = Markdown('ast').parse('> # Quote\n> text')[0]
print(quote['type'])
print([(child['type'], child.get('level'), child.get('text')) for child in quote['children']])

all_cases.description: Inline parsing handles emphasis and strong emphasis.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
children = Markdown('ast')('*em* and __strong__')[0]['children']
print([c['type'] for c in children])
print(children[0]['children'][0]['text'], children[2]['children'][0]['text'])

all_cases.description: Inline code spans are literal islands and suppress nested inline parsing.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
print(Markdown()('Use `**literal** <tag>` now'))

all_cases.description: Inline parsing handles links and image attributes.
all_cases.test_code: from minimarkdown import Markdown html = Markdown()('[*docs*](https://e.test?a=1&b=2 "T & Q") ![A *B*](img.png "Pic")') print('docs' in html, 'href="https://e.test?a=1&b=2"' in html, 'title="T & Q"' in html) print('' in html)

all_cases.description: Inline parsing handles escapes, autolinks, and hard line breaks.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown html = Markdown()('Escaped \\* star and \nnext\\\nline') print('Escaped * star' in html) print('https://example.test' in html) print(html.count('
') == 2)

all_cases.description: HTML rendering escapes literal text and attributes from direct public token dictionaries.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import HTMLRenderer, escape_html
print(escape_html('"<&>'))
html = HTMLRenderer().render([{'type':'paragraph','children':[{'type':'link','url':'https://e.test?a=1&b="q"','title':'A & B','children':[{'type':'text','text':'go'}]}]}])
print(html)

all_cases.description: HTML renderer emits core block tags, including heading anchors, from direct public token dictionaries.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import HTMLRenderer
tokens = [{'type':'heading','level':2,'attrs':{'id':'hi-there'},'children':[{'type':'text','text':'Hi'}]},{'type':'thematic_break'},{'type':'block_code','text':'x < y','lang':'py'}]
print(HTMLRenderer().render(tokens))

all_cases.description: AST renderer returns public token dictionaries with nested inline children and without private state.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
tokens = Markdown('ast')('Text **bold** and `code`')
para = tokens[0]
print(type(tokens).__name__, para['type'])
print([c['type'] for c in para['children']])
print(any(k.startswith('_') for t in tokens for k in t.keys()))

all_cases.description: The strikethrough plugin registers inline syntax and HTML rendering.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
print(Markdown(plugins=['strikethrough'])('~~gone & back~~'))

all_cases.description: The table plugin recognizes pipe table block shape and alignments.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
t = Markdown('ast', plugins=['table'])('| A | B | C |\n| :--- | :---: | ---: |\n| 1 | 2 | 3 |')[0]
print(t['type'], t['align'])
print([c['text'] for c in t['header']])
print([c['text'] for c in t['rows'][0]])

all_cases.description: The task list plugin recognizes checked and unchecked item markers.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
items = Markdown('ast', plugins=['task_list'])('- [x] done\n- [ ] todo')[0]['items']
print([(i.get('checked'), i.get('text')) for i in items])

all_cases.description: Custom inline plugin callables can register parse and render handlers.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown

def mark_plugin(md):
    md.register_inline('mark', r'==([^=]+)==', lambda m: {'text': m.group(1)}, lambda r, t: '<mark>' + r.render_inlines([{'type':'text','text':t['text']}]) + '</mark>')

print(Markdown(plugins=[mark_plugin])('A ==hit & run=='))

all_cases.description: Code spans inside prose and fenced code blocks both escape HTML while suppressing nested inline parsing.
all_cases.test_code: from minimarkdown import Markdown html = Markdown()('Inline `****` here.\n\n```html\n& raw\n```') print('**<x>**' in html) print('& raw' in html) print('<strong>& raw</strong>' in html)

all_cases.description: Link labels can contain nested inline emphasis while href and title attributes are escaped independently.
all_cases.test_code: from minimarkdown import Markdown html = Markdown()('[*A & B*](https://e.test?q=1&v="x" "T & C")') print('A & B' in html) print('href="https://e.test?q=1&v="x""' in html) print('title="T & C"' in html)

all_cases.description: Task-list checked state is plugin metadata while item text remains normal inline-parsed list content in AST and HTML.
all_cases.test_code: from minimarkdown import Markdown html = Markdown(plugins=['task_list'])('- [x] **Done**\n- [ ] [Todo](https://e.test)') ast = Markdown('ast', plugins=['task_list'])('- [x] **Done**\n- [ ] [Todo](https://e.test)') print(' Done' in html) print(' Todo' in html) print([item.get('checked') for item in ast[0]['items']])

all_cases.description: Custom plugin tokens recognized by the parser are visible to both HTML and AST renderers through the shared dispatch model.
all_cases.test_code: from minimarkdown import Markdown, escape_html def badge_plugin(md): md.register_inline('badge', r'\[\[([^\]]+)\]\]', lambda m: {'text': m.group(1)}, lambda r, t: '' + escape_html(t['text']) + '') html = Markdown(plugins=[badge_plugin])('Ship [[v1 & beta]]') ast = Markdown('ast', plugins=[badge_plugin])('Ship [[v1 & beta]]') print(html) print(ast[0]['children'][1]['type'], ast[0]['children'][1]['text'])

all_cases.description: Ambiguous table-looking text after a paragraph remains paragraph text unless delimiter order creates a table.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown m = Markdown(plugins=['table']) not_table = m('Intro paragraph\nA | B\n| --- | --- |') yes_table = m('| A | B |\n| --- | --- |\n| 1 | 2 |') print('' in not_table, '

Intro paragraph' in not_table) print('

' in yes_table, '' in yes_table)
12

all_cases.description: Malformed inline/plugin syntax remains literal, preserves later valid constructs, and does not corrupt later parses on the same parser instance.
all_cases.test_code: from minimarkdown import Markdown m = Markdown(plugins=['strikethrough']) first = m('Bad ~~strike and [link](oops then **ok**') second = m('Next ~~good~~ and *fine*') third = m.parse('Next ~~good~~ and *fine*') print('~~strike' in first, 'ok' in first) print(second) print([c['type'] for c in third[0]['children']])
